fact: May 17 is exactly 137 days into the year - that's almost exactly 4.5 months of the year already gone!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are going absolutely bonkers right now, turning into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow to make wishes!
This date falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, which means if the zodiac were a pizza, we'd be right in the cheesy, stubborn center slice!
Baby raccoons are just starting to peek out of their dens around this time in North America - they're about the size of hamsters and twice as mischievous!
The sun is climbing so high in northern countries that by May 17, some places get nearly 16 hours of daylight - that's like having summer bedtimes in spring!
Lilac bushes are exploding with purple flowers right now in temperate regions, and they smell so strong they can make your nose tingle from 20 feet away!
We're still about a month away from the summer solstice, but the days are getting longer by about 2 minutes every single day in most places!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere spots, May 17 means autumn is getting serious - leaves are turning colors and falling like nature's confetti!
Tadpoles in ponds across the Northern Hemisphere are starting to sprout tiny back legs around this time, looking like weird little swimming aliens!
The morning dew is getting heavier in many places because warm days and cool nights create the perfect recipe for grass that looks like it's covered in tiny diamonds!
May 17 sits right in the sweet spot when cherry trees have finished blooming but apple trees are still showing off their pink and white flowers!
Migrating butterflies like painted ladies are hitting their stride right now, traveling up to 100 miles per day on wings thinner than tissue paper!
